title: service-accounts — compliance evidence
description: Service accounts are the primary machine-credential surface — SOC 2 (CC6.1, CC6.7, CC7.2, CC7.3), NIST SP 800-131A (approved cryptographic algorithms), OWASP ASVS V6 (stored cryptography + access control), and PCI-DSS 8.6 (service-account authentication) all apply. Every enforcement is codified as CODE + AUDIT rather than a policy document; the compliance officer verifies via automated checks against the entries below.

controls.principle: logical access controls — every machine action correlated to an identity
controls.evidence: Every request presenting a SA JWT passes through `service_account.audit` middleware which writes: (a) `service_account.request.audit` activity_log row (method + path + status + service_account_id + application_id + tenant_id + ip_prefix_24 + user_agent + correlation_id + duration_ms), (b) `service_account.jwt_verified` activity_log row keyed by jti (replay-attack signal), (c) N× HasAudit rows per Eloquent write. Retention 365 days hot, 7 years cold.

Token-exchange endpoint rate-limited per-SA (5/min) + per-IP-/24 (20/min). Fingerprinter tracks (SA, ip_prefix_24, user_agent_hash) tuples over rolling 30 days; novel tuple + >5x baseline rate = SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification to security. Kid disable revokes every JWT signed under it, batched via cache invalidation.

SA deletion (soft) is immediate: `is_enabled=false` + `deleted_at=now()`. Every outstanding JWT's jti added to the deny-list cache. New token-exchange refused. Kid burn (via `identity/auth`) is the hard-revocation lever for shared kids.

DetectDormantServiceAccountJob (weekly) reports SAs unused > 30d. `academorix.service_account.dormant_count` gauge feeds monitoring pipeline. First-request-after-dormancy fires `ServiceAccountUsageDetected` (distinct signal for compromise-adjacent wake-ups). Suspicious-activity fingerprint detection covered under CC6.6.

controls.principle: vulnerability evaluation on account credential lifecycle
controls.evidence: Mandatory rotation via `expires_at` (default 90d TTL). NotifyRotationDueJob (weekly) dispatches 14-day warning. Grace period 7d after `expires_at`; PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob (daily) then soft-disables. `service-accounts.rotation.notify_emails` config for team-wide notifications.

controls.principle: approved cryptographic algorithms — HS256 requires ≥ 32-byte key
controls.evidence: Every entry in `auth::jwt_signing_keys` for `algorithm=HS256` MUST have `secret_material` ≥ 32 bytes (256 bits). Enforced at key creation by the auth module's `JwtSigningKeyProvisioner`. This module only NAMES the kid — the key-length constraint is verified upstream. Health probe `service-account-hs256-key-configured` fails production boot if any active kid violates the length.
`SecretRotator` uses `Hash::make($plain, ['rounds' => 12])`. Cost bumps roadmap alongside CPU-cost benchmarking; the module never regresses below 12.
JWT signing routed through `auth::JwtSigner`; that module wraps `firebase/php-jwt` (audited implementation). This module never signs directly.
Per-SA `signer_kid` isolation; kid rotation via `auth::JwtSigningKeyRotator`; kid burn revokes all JWTs signed under it. Rotation-hygiene dashboard tracks mean days-since-rotation per SA.

Token-exchange endpoint requires TLS (Foundation-level `cors.strict` + `enforce.tls` middleware). Bearer JWT header only — no query-string credential. `Authorization: Bearer` scheme only — no `Basic` scheme (SAs use a stronger primitive than shared HTTP Basic).

`secret_hash` is bcrypt at cost 12. `mfa_secret_encrypted`-style KMS wrapping is intentionally NOT used — SA secrets are single-purpose bearer credentials that rotate frequently; the bcrypt hash is one-way + sufficient. Plaintext secret returned ONCE at creation + rotation, then irretrievable.

controls.principle: service accounts must not use interactive login
controls.evidence: SAs have NO login page, NO MFA, NO password-reset. Every credential presentation is programmatic via the token-exchange endpoint. Interactive login is architecturally impossible.

controls.principle: service account credentials cannot be embedded in code
controls.evidence: This is a POLICY control not enforced by this module — documented in the ops runbook (Doppler secrets, K8s secrets, Vault dynamic secrets). The module contributes by: (a) plaintext-secret-shown-once discipline that discourages code embedding, (b) rotation cadence that surfaces embedded credentials via rotation-noncompliance dashboards.
Mandatory `expires_at` (default 90d TTL). NotifyRotationDueJob dispatches 14-day warnings. PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob soft-disables past-grace SAs.
